english_explanations: Able to be changed or adapted to suit different conditions or purposes.
example_sentences: The adjustable chair allows employees to work comfortably throughout the day.

english_explanations: Able to move quickly and easily; able to think and understand quickly.
example_sentences: Agile companies can respond to market changes faster than their competitors.

english_explanations: An amount of money given regularly to someone; an amount allowed or permitted.
example_sentences: Employees receive a travel allowance for business trips.

english_explanations: A short amusing or interesting story about a real incident or person.
example_sentences: The speaker opened with a humorous anecdote about his first day at the company.

english_explanations: An object that is old and valuable, often collected because of its age and quality.
example_sentences: The antique furniture in the conference room adds a touch of elegance.

english_explanations: The way someone or something looks; an act of appearing in public.
example_sentences: Professional appearance is important when meeting clients for the first time.

english_explanations: In a way that is suitable or proper for a particular situation.
example_sentences: Dress appropriately for the formal dinner with our international partners.

english_explanations: To awaken a feeling or emotion; to stimulate interest or attention.
example_sentences: The presentation failed to arouse any interest from the potential investors.

english_explanations: Something valuable that a person or company owns; a useful or valuable quality.
example_sentences: Her multilingual skills are a major asset to the international team.

english_explanations: To connect or link something with something else in one's mind; a partner or colleague.
example_sentences: I associate this brand with high quality and reliability.

english_explanations: Clothing, especially of a particular type or for a particular occasion.
example_sentences: Business attire is required for the annual shareholders meeting.

english_explanations: The act of getting on a ship, aircraft, or other vehicle.
example_sentences: Boarding will begin in ten minutes at Gate 12.

english_explanations: A place where beer is made commercially.
example_sentences: The brewery exports its products to over twenty countries worldwide.

english_explanations: Short in duration or length; a concise summary of a case or situation.
example_sentences: Please keep your presentation brief as we have limited time.

english_explanations: An estimate of income and expenditure for a set period; to plan spending.
example_sentences: We need to stay within the budget for this marketing campaign.

english_explanations: A series of organized activities to achieve a particular goal, especially in business or politics.
example_sentences: The advertising campaign successfully increased brand awareness.

english_explanations: Relaxed and informal; not formal or official.
example_sentences: The office has a casual dress code on Fridays.

english_explanations: A list of items required or things to be done, used as a reminder.
example_sentences: Use the checklist to ensure all documents are prepared before the audit.

english_explanations: A person or organization using the services of a professional company.
example_sentences: The client was satisfied with the proposal we submitted.

english_explanations: A promise to do something or behave in a particular way; dedication to a cause.
example_sentences: His commitment to quality has earned him a strong reputation.

english_explanations: In a brief and clear manner, expressing much in few words.
example_sentences: Please summarize the report concisely for the executive meeting.

english_explanations: The end or finish of an event or process; a judgment or decision reached after consideration.
example_sentences: In conclusion, the project exceeded all expectations.

english_explanations: The manner in which a person behaves; to organize and carry out an activity.
example_sentences: The company will conduct a survey to measure customer satisfaction.

english_explanations: A formal meeting for discussion, typically lasting several days.
example_sentences: The annual sales conference will be held in Singapore next month.

english_explanations: Feeling sure about one's own abilities or qualities; having trust in someone or something.
example_sentences: She appeared confident during her presentation to the board.

english_explanations: Aware of and responding to one's surroundings; having knowledge of something.
example_sentences: We need to be conscious of cultural differences when negotiating.

english_explanations: Based on or in accordance with what is generally done or believed.
example_sentences: Conventional methods may not work in this innovative market.

english_explanations: Relating to a large company or group; relating to all members of a group.
example_sentences: Corporate headquarters are located in the financial district.

english_explanations: To match or be in agreement with something; to communicate by letter or email.
example_sentences: The results correspond with our initial predictions.

english_explanations: The showing of politeness in one's attitude and behavior toward others.
example_sentences: It is common courtesy to arrive on time for business meetings.

english_explanations: A formal or explicit statement or announcement; an act of declaring something.
example_sentences: A customs declaration is required for all imported goods.

english_explanations: To announce something clearly and officially; to state something firmly.
example_sentences: You must declare any items of value at the border.

english_explanations: Behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety; proper conduct.
example_sentences: Maintaining decorum is essential during formal diplomatic events.

english_explanations: A person sent to represent others; to entrust a task or responsibility to another person.
example_sentences: The manager decided to delegate the project to her most experienced team member.

english_explanations: The action of leaving, especially to start a journey; a deviation from an accepted course.
example_sentences: The departure time for the flight has been delayed by two hours.

english_explanations: Successful and respected; having a dignified and impressive appearance.
example_sentences: The distinguished professor was invited to speak at the conference.

english_explanations: An agent or company that supplies goods to stores or other businesses.
example_sentences: We are looking for a new distributor in the Asian market.

english_explanations: Existing or occurring inside a particular country; relating to the home or family.
example_sentences: Domestic sales have increased significantly this quarter.

english_explanations: The process of cleaning clothes with chemical solvents rather than water.
example_sentences: This suit requires dry-cleaning rather than machine washing.

english_explanations: Subject to import or export duties; liable to customs tax.
example_sentences: All dutiable goods must be declared at customs.

english_explanations: The necessary items for a particular purpose, especially tools or machinery.
example_sentences: All office equipment will be upgraded next month.

english_explanations: Absolutely necessary; extremely important; the fundamental elements of something.
example_sentences: Effective communication is essential for successful teamwork.

english_explanations: To set up on a firm or permanent basis; to achieve recognition or acceptance.
example_sentences: The company plans to establish a new branch in Tokyo.

english_explanations: The customary code of polite behavior in society or among members of a particular profession.
example_sentences: Business etiquette varies significantly across different cultures.

english_explanations: An amount that is more than necessary or permitted; exceeding the permitted amount.
example_sentences: Excess baggage will incur additional charges at the airport.

english_explanations: More than is necessary, normal, or desirable; unreasonable or disproportionate.
example_sentences: Excessive spending on office supplies reduced the department's budget.

english_explanations: Only; without any others being included or involved.
example_sentences: This product line is exclusively available through our online store.

english_explanations: A large public exhibition or show; a comprehensive explanation of an idea or theory.
example_sentences: The trade exposition attracted exhibitors from over fifty countries.

english_explanations: Lacking restraint in spending money or using resources; costing too much money.
example_sentences: The extravagant party raised concerns about the company's spending habits.

english_explanations: To close or secure something firmly; to attach or fix something in place.
example_sentences: Please fasten your seatbelt before takeoff.

english_explanations: A journey made by air; the act of flying through the air.
example_sentences: Our flight to London departs at 9:00 AM.

english_explanations: A thing that is done in accordance with rules or customs; the rigid observance of rules.
example_sentences: The signing ceremony was merely a formality after the agreement was reached.

english_explanations: Regularly or habitually; often.
example_sentences: The manager frequently travels to the overseas branches.

english_explanations: An unintentional act or remark causing embarrassment to its originator.
example_sentences: His gaffe during the negotiations cost the company the deal.

english_explanations: A formal promise or assurance that certain conditions will be fulfilled; to provide such a promise.
example_sentences: All products come with a one-year guarantee against manufacturing defects.

english_explanations: A piece of printed information provided free of charge, especially to accompany a lecture or presentation.
example_sentences: Please review the handout before the next training session.

english_explanations: The number of people present in a group or organization; the act of counting people.
example_sentences: The headcount for the department has grown by twenty percent this year.

english_explanations: Having the main offices or headquarters in a particular place.
example_sentences: The company is headquartered in New York but operates globally.

english_explanations: An idea, feeling, or opinion about something or someone; an effect produced on someone.
example_sentences: First impressions are crucial during a job interview.

english_explanations: An official employed to examine and ensure compliance with regulations or standards.
example_sentences: The health inspector will visit the facility next week.

english_explanations: To fill someone with the urge or ability to do or feel something; to breathe in.
example_sentences: The CEO's speech inspired the team to achieve their quarterly goals.

english_explanations: Detailed information telling how something should be done or operated; the act of teaching.
example_sentences: Please follow the instructions carefully when assembling the equipment.

english_explanations: Of or situated on the inside; existing or occurring within an organization.
example_sentences: The memo is for internal use only and should not be shared externally.

english_explanations: To stop the continuous progress of an activity or process; to break into a conversation.
example_sentences: Please do not interrupt the speaker during the presentation.

english_explanations: To cause or enable a condition or state to continue; to keep in good condition.
example_sentences: It is important to maintain accurate records for all transactions.

english_explanations: To combine or cause to combine to form a single entity.
example_sentences: The two companies decided to merge to increase market share.

english_explanations: A company operating in several countries; involving or operating in several countries.
example_sentences: Working for a multinational corporation offers opportunities for international travel.

english_explanations: A thing aimed at or sought; not influenced by personal feelings or opinions.
example_sentences: The primary objective of this campaign is to increase brand awareness.

english_explanations: A particular event or instance; a special or noteworthy event or celebration.
example_sentences: Formal attire is appropriate for this special occasion.

english_explanations: To fail to notice something; to have a view from above; to choose to ignore.
example_sentences: Do not overlook the importance of proper documentation.

english_explanations: A distinct period or stage in a process of change or development.
example_sentences: The project is currently in the testing phase.

english_explanations: Not decorated or elaborate; simple or ordinary in character; clear and easy to understand.
example_sentences: Please use plain language in the report so everyone can understand.

english_explanations: A speech or talk in which a new product or idea is shown and explained; the manner of presenting something.
example_sentences: She delivered an impressive presentation to the board of directors.

english_explanations: Inspiring respect and admiration; having high status or reputation.
example_sentences: He graduated from a prestigious university with honors.

english_explanations: In a manner relating to or belonging to a profession; in a competent and skillful manner.
example_sentences: Always behave professionally when representing the company.

english_explanations: An outline of something, especially a person's face, as seen from one side; a short description of someone or something.
example_sentences: Please update your company profile on the website.

english_explanations: A financial gain, especially the difference between the amount earned and the amount spent.
example_sentences: The company reported a significant increase in profit this quarter.

english_explanations: A line or sequence of people or vehicles awaiting their turn to be attended to or to proceed.
example_sentences: Please join the queue at the registration desk.

english_explanations: A formal statement of the estimated cost of goods or services; a group of words taken from a text or speech.
example_sentences: We requested a quotation for the new office furniture.

english_explanations: A written or printed statement acknowledging that something has been paid for or received.
example_sentences: Please keep your receipt for any returns or exchanges.

english_explanations: The action or process of registering or being registered; an entry in a register.
example_sentences: Conference registration closes two weeks before the event.

english_explanations: Over and over again; constantly or frequently.
example_sentences: She repeatedly emphasized the importance of meeting deadlines.

english_explanations: To stand for or be a symbol of something; to act or speak on behalf of someone.
example_sentences: He was chosen to represent the company at the international summit.

english_explanations: A person chosen or appointed to act or speak for another or others; typical of a particular group.
example_sentences: Our sales representative will contact you within two business days.

english_explanations: The beliefs or opinions that are generally held about someone or something; the state of being held in high esteem.
example_sentences: The company has built a strong reputation for quality and reliability.

english_explanations: An arrangement to have something held for one's use; a feeling of doubt or uncertainty about something.
example_sentences: I made a reservation at the restaurant for seven o'clock.

english_explanations: Income, especially when of a company or organization and of a substantial nature.
example_sentences: Annual revenue increased by fifteen percent compared to last year.

english_explanations: Strong and healthy; vigorous; able to withstand difficult conditions.
example_sentences: The robust economy supported strong business growth.

english_explanations: Having an uneven or irregular surface; not exact or precise; difficult or unpleasant.
example_sentences: This is just a rough estimate; we will provide exact figures later.

english_explanations: The state of being free from danger or threat; measures taken to protect against espionage or crime.
example_sentences: Security measures have been tightened at all office locations.

english_explanations: To attempt to find or obtain something; to ask for something from someone.
example_sentences: We are seeking qualified candidates for the marketing position.

english_explanations: To resolve or reach an agreement about something; to establish a home or business; to pay a debt.
example_sentences: We hope to settle the dispute through negotiation rather than litigation.

english_explanations: Firm and stable in shape; having a strong basis; reliable and dependable.
example_sentences: The team has a solid track record of delivering projects on time.

english_explanations: Having or displaying a good sense of style; fashionable and attractive.
example_sentences: The office features a stylish design that impresses visitors.

english_explanations: Higher in rank, status, or quality; better than average or better than others of a similar kind.
example_sentences: Our product offers superior performance compared to competitors.

english_explanations: Relating to a particular subject, art, or craft, or its techniques; involving specialized knowledge.
example_sentences: The report contains technical details that may require expert review.

english_explanations: Easy to use or understand, especially for people who are not experts.
example_sentences: The new software is designed to be user-friendly for beginners.

english_explanations: An unoccupied position or job; an empty space or room available for use.
example_sentences: We have a vacancy in the accounting department for a senior analyst.

english_explanations: Not occupied; empty; showing no intelligence or interest.
example_sentences: The office next door has been vacant for three months.

english_explanations: Relating to seeing or sight; a picture or piece of film used to illustrate a talk or presentation.
example_sentences: The presentation included visual aids to help explain the data.
